entries.title: On Galatians 3:1
entries.excerpt: Verse 1 O foolish Galatians who did bewitch you, before whose eyes Jesus Christ was openly set forth, crucified? Here he passes to another subject; in the former chapters he had shown himself not to be an Apostle of men, nor by men, nor in want of Apostolic instruction. Now, having established his authority as a teacher, he proceeds to discourse more confidently, and draws a comparison between faith and the Law. At the outset he said, I marvel that you are so quickly removing; Galatians 1:6 but here, O foolish Galatians; then, his indignation was in its birth, but now, after his refutation of…
